raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the review focus primarily on technical argument validity, writing polish, or both?","rationale":{"R1.2":"A generic section review centrally includes checking whether the stated benchmark results support the argumentative progression to the deployment claim.","R1.3":"The artifact makes multiple quantitative performance and cost claims, so reviewing whether the reported evidence supports those claims is central.","R1.6":"Because the delegation asks to review a section, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision are expected responsibilities.","RX.1":"The reviewer should disclose limits caused by reviewing only the provided section without external benchmark or paper context.","RX.2":"The section contains broad dominance and strongest-system claims, making overclaim avoidance a load-bearing review concern.","RX.3":"The review must stay within the provided section and not perform unrelated rewriting or external validation unless requested.","RX.4":"Misleading production-readiness or dominance claims could affect downstream deployment decisions, so harm-aware review is active.","RX.5":"Any review judgment should be traceable to specific statements or numbers in the provided artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus primarily on technical argument validity, writing polish, or both?

rationale.R1.2: A generic section review centrally includes checking whether the stated benchmark results support the argumentative progression to the deployment claim.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes multiple quantitative performance and cost claims, so reviewing whether the reported evidence supports those claims is central.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks to review a section, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision are expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer should disclose limits caused by reviewing only the provided section without external benchmark or paper context.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains broad dominance and strongest-system claims, making overclaim avoidance a load-bearing review concern.
rationale.RX.3: The review must stay within the provided section and not perform unrelated rewriting or external validation unless requested.
rationale.RX.4: Misleading production-readiness or dominance claims could affect downstream deployment decisions, so harm-aware review is active.
rationale.RX.5: Any review judgment should be traceable to specific statements or numbers in the provided artifact.
